# NSMergePolicyType.errorMergePolicyType

**Case**

The default merge policy for all managed object contexts.

## Declaration

```swift
case errorMergePolicyType
```

## Discussion

If a save fails because of conflicting objects, you can find the IDs of those objects in error’s `userInfo` dictionary. Use the [doc://com.apple.coredata/documentation/CoreData/NSInsertedObjectsKey] and [doc://com.apple.coredata/documentation/CoreData/NSUpdatedObjectsKey] keys to extract the object IDs.

## Policies

- **NSMergePolicyType.mergeByPropertyStoreTrumpMergePolicyType**: A property-based merge policy that applies external changes.
- **NSMergePolicyType.mergeByPropertyObjectTrumpMergePolicyType**: A property-based merge policy that applies in-memory changes.
- **NSMergePolicyType.overwriteMergePolicyType**: A merge policy type that overwrites the entire stored object.
- **NSMergePolicyType.rollbackMergePolicyType**: A merge policy that discards unsaved changes.
